steps.title: Identify the cost tier for each class
steps.content:
Map the promotion's pricing by class order:
  • 1st class: Free = \$0
  • 2nd class: Half off = \$22.80 ÷ 2 = \$11.40
  • Classes 3 and beyond: Regular price = \$22.80 each
Since we take x classes total, the number of regularly-priced classes is (x − 2).

steps.title: Build the total cost formula
steps.content:
Sum costs across all tiers:
$$f(x) = 0 + 11.40 + 22.80(x-2)$$
Rearrange:
$$f(x) = 22.80(x-2) + 11.40$$
Verify with x = 3: $f(3) = 22.80(1) + 11.40 = 22.80 + 11.40 = 34.20$. This matches taking 1 free class (\$0) + 1 discounted class (\$11.40) + 1 full-price class (\$22.80) = \$34.20. The answer is A.
